pages.name: p. 208 - Welcome back, adventurers! This is a summary of all changes made in the sec

text.content:
Original PDF page 208

Welcome back, adventurers! This is a summary of all changes made in the second Alpha update. It focuses mainly on
Characters and 5.5e compatibility. Remember to submit your feedback through the Google Form–feedback sent
through other channels has a high chance of being missed!

Changelog Alpha v.1.2

Here be Legends

General text improvements throughout the chapter.

Edition Compatibility. [NEW] Added edition
compatibility note.

Multiplier Attack Limitations. [NEW] Added a
limitations section to avoid confusion on Multiplier

Legendary Characters

Now 5.5e compatible! New features everywhere to account for changes between editions. Maybe we missed something
about 5.5e! Do point it out on the forms document.

Barbarian

Devastating Strike. [REWORKED] Now deals less
damage but Impairs a target. Brutal Strike integration
from 5.5e!

Legendary Rage. [NEW] New feature, Legendary
Rage!

Legendary Endurance. [REMOVED] No longer a
Player Character feature.

Barbaric Leap. [NEW] Formerly an exclusive
Barbarian Mythical Technique from the Durandal
school, now a staple of all Barbarians to increaase
mobility.

Earthbreaker. [REMOVED] Replaced by Barbaric
Leap. Earthbreaker is now a Mythical Technique from
the Durandal school!

Challenging Roar. [BUFFED] Now compels creatures
to attack the Barbarian but has reduced range.

Effortless Resistance. Now gained at 24th level
instead of at 22nd.
Bard

Legendary Inspiration. Increased range but lowered
its effectiveness.

Masterful Inspiration. [NEW] Bardic Inspiration die
now 1d20 at 23rd level and above. Bardic Inspiration
can be used to cast spells as a bonus action, freeing up
the action economy of the Bard.

Master of All Trades. [REMOVED] Replaced by
Absolute Inspiration.
Cleric

Burst of Divinity. [NERFED] Now requires an action
instead of a bonus action, but has a x5 multiplier

CHANGELOG

Attacks.

Lethal Damage. Does not affect Multiplier Attacks in
the same way, but rather adds a die to the Multiplier
Attack’s equation.

instead of a x4 multiplier for both healing and damage.

Warded by a Higher Power. [NERFED] Now breaks
upon casting any spell instead of only damaging spells.

Blessed Recovery. [BUFFED] Now regains all
channel divinity uses.
Druid

Verdant Crown. [BUFFED] Now gives a much-needed
spellcasting bonus to concentration-type druid builds!
May be too strong, as it comes quite early in the
progression.

Wild Shape. 5.5e wild shape integrations on several
abilities (Improved Legendary Forms, Final Form)

Rejuvenating Recovery. [BUFFED] Now restores a
use of Wild Shape.
Fighter [Major Changes]

Perfect Strike. [REWORKED] Now scales with
forgone attacks.

Improved Second Wind. [BUFFED] More uses in line
with 5.5e design. Still powerful in 5e.

Legendary Warrior. Moved to 22nd level.

Quality Build. [REMOVED] Confusing feature, not
flavored for all fighters.

Legendary Athlete. [REMOVED] Not impactful
enough and confusing, especially on a class with so
many Mythical Technique options.

Legendary Action. Now available at 23rd level, but
less effective (or more, depending on your ruling) when
it comes to attacks. Can only be used once per round.

Powerful Reserve. [NEW] New 24th level feature.

pages.name: p. 209 - Extra Attack x4. [REMOVED] Fighter can no longer

text.content:
Original PDF page 209

Extra Attack x4. [REMOVED] Fighter can no longer
attack 5 times at 25th level. Difficult to balance,
disregarded the importance of Multiplier Attacks.

Kensei Technique. [NEW] Added Kensei Techniques
back into the game! Previous iteration of this feature
conflicted with Warlord Heroic Path.
Monk [Major Changes]

Body, Soul, Mind. Added 5.5e feature (Body and
Mind) to 5e monks at 20th level, along with Superior
Defense. General 5.5e compatibility across the class.

World Strike. Now deals Soul damage.

Ki-Borne Awareness. [BUFFED] Now grants
truesight, lasts for a minute.

Mythical Techniques. [NERFED] Monk no longer has
access to 3 Mythical Techniques, but rather 2. Balanced
for 5.5e Monks.

Instinct of Deflection. [REMOVED] Didn’t come into
play at all; couldn’t be adapted for 5.5e compatibility.

Masterful Deflection. [NEW] New feature. LP
Spender that allows the Monk to resist incoming
damage (akin to a Legendary Endurance).

Supleness of Wind & River. [REWORKED] Now
allows the Monk to fly. Previous version never came
into play.

Reactive. [NEW] Monk gains an additional reaction at
23rd level. Powerful effect, allows for greater deflection.
Paladin

Legendary Smite. [REWORKED] Now scales with
spell slot expended, like a true smite should.

Oathborn Miracle/Greater Oathborn Miracle.
[BUFFED] Paladin now has 6th and 7th-level spell
slots.

Immortal Challenger. [NEW] Powerful high-level LP
spender for the Paladin.

Ranger [Major Changes]

Incapacitating Strike. [NEW] Slaying strike replaced
by Incapacitating strike. More focused on control and
Lethal damage. Critical hit on a bloodied creature is still
potentially better than other Multiplier Attacks.

Expert Hunter. Different features for different editions.

Slayer’s Mark. Now applies Hunter’s Mark for 5.5e
compatibility.

Greater Awareness. [NEW] Replaces Greater
Primeval Awareness.

Killing Strikes. Now a 23rd level feature. Gives lethal
damage on a critical hit.

Frontierless Ranger. [REMOVED] For 5.5e
compatibility.

Unseen Tracker. [REWORKED] Now grants
nondetection and synergizes with the Invisible condition
(5.5e)

Primeval Burst. [BUFFED] Is now added to the
damage of an attack instead of replacing it.

Favored Strike. [REMOVED] Added into Slayer of
Legends
Rogue [Major Changes]

Mortal Blow. [REWORKED] In line with the feedback
received by a certain anonymous backer, Mortal Blow
now folded into Sneak Attack progression.

Legendary Sneak Attack. [NEW] Greater Sneak
Attack scaling.

Heightened Senses. Adapted for 5.5e.

Irrepressible Mind. [REWORKED] No longer
interacts with Legendary Resistances.

Reactive. [NEW] Same feature as the Monk! Allows for
greater use of Legendary Reaction.

Sneak Attack Master. [NEW] Replaces Improved
Mortal Blow. Allows for 2 Mortal Blows in a single
round, and one additional Sneak Attack during the
Rogue’s turn.

Cunning Recovery. [NEW] Rogue can now use
Cunning Action while recovering.

Asbolute Finesse. Moved to 24th level.
Sorcerer

Improved Sorcery. [NEW] New feature. Brings 5e and
5.5e in line.

Greater Metamagic Master. [NEW] Up to 2 G.
Metamagic options in a single spell and 10th-level spell
quality addition.

Sorcerous Restoarion. [NEW] Sorcerer recovers
Sorcery Points with their Recover action, giving them
virtually infinite lower level spell slots outside of
combat.

Magical Sustenance. [NEW] Gives Sorcerers a
powerful sustain tool.

Wellspring of Arcana. [REWORKED] Now gives 1
free use of Greater Metamagic per turn.

Unendurable Spell. [BUFFED] Now makes one
target fail a saving throw automatically.
 

CHANGELOG

Original PDF page 210

Warlock [MAJOR CHANGES]

Legendary Invocations. [NEW] Highly requested
feature. Go nuts. They might be too powerful; we’ll have
to playtest them.

Persistent Arcahnum. [REWORKED] No longer a
random spell slot generator!

Patron’s Influence. Moved to 22nd level, buffed to be
Charisma instead of a flat modifier!

Patron’s Predilection. [BUFFED] Now allows the
Warlock to avoid an instance of damage entirely. This
can be combo’d with Theoprastus’ Flux, which is now
available to warlocks also. If someone pulls that off,
please send me a message.

Eldritch Invocation Savant. [NEW] Tired of not
being able to change your invocations? Here, you can

Heroic Paths

Some changes indeed! Balance updates mainly.

Chosen One. [NERFED] Convergence only lasts for 1
hour and gives 1 advantage, not 3. Chosen One (tier III)
nerfed in both its features.

Shadowblade. [BUFFED] Death From Nowhere 500
damage cap removed… Don’t make me regret this?

Mythical Techniques

Mythical Techniques Table. All techniques are easy
to reference in this table. Remmeber to check the
weapon requirements! You cannot use Critical Edge
with a ranged weapon and have Legendary Sniper grant
you crits on all weapon attacks.

Soul Strike. [NERFED] Now costs 2 LP.

White Flame. [REWORKED] Not really flavorful in a
school meant for singular strikes. Reworked to grant
Soul damage to a single attack, even a Multiplier Attack.

Divine Parry. [BUFFED] No longer requires
concentration.

Blasphemous Hand. [BUFFED] Now available to
Barbarians. Deals 20d6 damage instead of 15d6 and
grants full damage temporary hit point barrier!

Earthbreaker. [NEW] Replaces Barbaric Leap!

Obliterating Strike. [NERFED] Alright, too many
Obliterating Strike crazy combos. They are fun, but they
should not be easy to pull off. You can now use this
technique only ONCE per day! Use it well…

Anatomical Control. [BUFFED] Rephrased to
encourage use before battle. No longer requires

CHANGELOG

now customize your warlock to your heart’s content! On
a serious note, this might need some playtesting,
sounds fun but it may break some games.

Supernatural Intervention. [REWORKED] Now
lasts for an entire day, so you can summon your patron
before a battle, but can only be used once per week.
Wizard

Efficient Flow. [BUFFED] Now grants an additional
concentration. Yeah, go nuts.

*** Greater Magicker.*** [NEW] Increases spell save DC
and makes your magic harder to modify!

Memorized Spellbook. [NEW] I’m really happy with
this one. It just feels like something only a Wizard
would do.

Warlord. [NERFED] Nerfed across the board, but also
buffed! Technique Student Heroic Perk now an easy
way to get a new Mythical Technique at 22nd level for
many classes that don’t have them. Warlord final
feature has been reduced in effectiveness, no longer will
you have 0-cost Mythical Techniques.

Concentration!

Apparate. Now available to Rangers.

pages.name: p. 211 - Just one change; Lord of Anguish’s Pulse of Anguish, its most powerful abil

text.content:
Original PDF page 211

Just one change; Lord of Anguish’s Pulse of Anguish, its most powerful ability, now costs 4 LP. Yes, a heavy change to
make, but that ability does go off as intended; it can very well stop a party dead in its tracks, allowing for the Lord of
Anguish to recover its LP regardless. It wiped a couple of playtester parties…

Epic Magic

Minor wordingadjustments to several spells.

Theophrastus’ Flux. [BUFFED] Someone did the
math, and this spell was not very good per action spent.
It is now much more powerful if it reaches full charge,

Legendary Foes

What’s in the Works?

New section! Here are some of the things that I couldn’t quite finish or that are upcoming in future versions of the
Alpha! We discuss design choices and future BoL content on our Discord almost every Thursday!

Legendary Characters. Starblade and Shaper are still pending! We want to first have a very solid base on all
Legendary Characters before we add our own homebrew classes.

Heroic Paths. Heroic Paths need a thorough look, especially some of the least-playtested ones. Heroic Paths will
probably have their own Alpha update, focused on adding more Heroic Paths and creating many more connections
between paths, if possible. We’ve found that not many people jump between paths. Perhaps it isn’t a very sought-after
feature.

Mythical Techniques. Several new Mythical Technique schools are in the works. Will probably have its own update!
Mythical Techniques are still not quite where we want them to be: some people have created absolutely broken
combos that annihilate even the Hermit (with over 2000+ damage), while others have mentioned that some Mythical
Techniques are not very usable during combat. We have to tune them a bit more!

Epic Magic. I’ve had very few comments about Epic Magic. Mostly, these spells seem to be very hard to pull off, but
very rewarding, with some exceptions. That is our goal! A future update will add more Epic Spells.

The Legendary Game. Pretty much covered! Maybe a few sections will be added in the future on how to improvise
and adapt to a Legendary Party that may break all rules.

Legendary Foes. Many Legendary Foes already in the works! 5 new foes have been completed in the last few weeks,
and all of them possess a new “Difficulty Scaling” section, for DMs with truly unhinged parties. We want these fights
to be hard, after all! Think of it as the Challenge mode of the book. We may add a scaling to reduce a Foe’s power level,
also.

Relics. In the works!

Appendices. Also in the works!

but restricts the caster’s turns heavily. Pull it off, and
you’ll be a hero!

True Wish. Minor wording adjustments.

pages.name: p. 212 - Hey guys! Marce here. I’ve been working on the balance of this book for a w

text.content:
Original PDF page 212

Hey guys! Marce here. I’ve been working on the balance of this book for a while now, even before we launched the
Kickstarter. Your help has been invaluable – thank you, genuinely.

This v1.2 brings sweeping changes to many of the classes, with a focus on 5.5e compatibility across the board. That
many changes means the game might actually be more unbalanced than before, which is a sentence I never thought I’d
have to write. Most of what you’ll find across the document is aimed at ensuring each class identity carries properly
into the legendary levels, so if your Fighter suddenly feels like a completely different beast, that’s intentional. Probably.

Fighters, Monks, Rangers, Rogues, and Warlocks got MAJOR changes. This means they need playtesting BADLY.
Consider rolling one of these classes in your next Alpha adventure!

I want to thank our incredible playtesters, who have been running mock legendary battles in the Discord server and
catching things we never would have caught on our own: Yogurttank (an editor of the book, actually!), Kalamish,
Faction (AKA Barbatos), Jayded, Spacebattler, Orfian, Seaburn, Ghostbot, Skyvrr, Memes4Life, Sol, aajack4484,
dodojesus, Kuro, Bun, and many others (very sorry if I am forgetting someone here!!). Y’all are the reason this book is
getting better. Come join us on the server if you want to give more detailed feedback–the main action is always there.

Dev Notes – Alpha v.1.2

There’s one particular anonymous playtester who has been playing with our Legendary Rules since before we even
launched this Kickstarter. They created an incredibly detailed 50-page PDF dissertation of each part of the Alpha,
simply named “Book of Legends Feedback Stuff” which greatly helped me put some things in perspective and conduct
some of these changes. I want to thank that person, especially!

Just know, dear reader, that if you are ever in the mood to write a 60-page anonymous paper on every facet of this
book, I will be reading it thoroughly! Send it to our Support inbox.

There is still plenty to refine before this Alpha is ready to move forward. Keep finding those broken combinations!
We’re going to make the best book ever.

– Marce, from the Codex of Strings

P.S: I made a copy of the previous feedback form to separate responses from the first two Alpha versions and this one.
Feel free to send a new survey, even if you already gave your feedback previously! Click or scan the QR code to leave
comments about the Alpha v.1.2 version.

CHANGELOG

Click Here or scan the QR code

to leave your feedback!
